meta.model_note: Probabilities come from simulating the whole tournament many times from current team strength; they update as real results come in.

routes.own_form: Win group: typically 6 pts (2W 0D 1L); from 0 pts now, ~6 more from the last 2
Runner-up: typically 6 pts (2W 0D 1L); as low as 3 pts in our simulations; from 0 pts now, ~6 more from the last 2
Best third: typically 4 pts (1W 1D 1L); as low as 3 pts in our simulations; from 0 pts now, ~4 more from the last 2

narration.headline: Uzbekistan face an uphill climb after opening defeat
narration.body: Uzbekistan face a challenging road ahead after their opening 3-1 defeat to Colombia. They are currently sitting at the bottom of Group K with zero points, leaving them adrift of the pace. To keep their hopes alive, they have real ground to make up on Congo DR, who sit just ahead of them with a 61% chance of advancing. Our tournament simulation gives Uzbekistan a 40% chance to reach the knockouts, meaning they face an uphill battle but remain very much in the fight. Their cleanest route is to chase a top-two finish, which is currently about a one-in-four shot. To get there, they likely need to find at least four points from their remaining two matches against Portugal and Congo DR. If they cannot secure a top-two spot, they have a slim back-door route to the Round of 32 by finishing as one of the best third-placed teams, though that path would leave their fate in the hands of results in other groups.

narration.headline: USA in Prime Position, but Australia Keep the Pressure High
narration.body: The United States are in a very strong position to reach the knockouts, sitting on a 93% chance to advance after opening with a convincing 4-1 win over Paraguay. However, they cannot afford to relax just yet. They lead Group D on goal difference, but Australia are right on their heels with a 92% chance to go through, setting up a tight race for the top spot. The main route for the Americans is to finish in the top two, which our tournament simulation sees as a strong chance. They can secure this by taking care of their own results in the remaining matches, with four more points likely enough to win the group. If things take an unexpected turn, there is still a slim back-door route to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, though that path would leave their fate in the hands of results in other groups. For now, the U.S. control their own path to the Round of 32.

narration.headline: Uruguay Well Placed in Group H After Opening Draw
narration.body: Uruguay are in a strong position to reach the knockouts, with our tournament simulation giving them a 69% chance to advance to the Round of 32. Following their opening 1-1 draw with Saudi Arabia, they sit in a tight Group H race where Spain lead the pack at 81% and Cape Verde Islands remain right behind at 59%. Because every team in the group drew their opener, Uruguay are currently tied at the top of the table with one point. Their cleanest route forward is finishing in the top two, which is more likely than not. To secure that direct path, they will likely need to find at least three more points from their remaining two matches. While there is a slim back-door route to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, that path is out of their hands and would leave them relying on results in other groups. For now, Uruguay's destiny remains very much in their own control.

narration.headline: Türkiye Facing a Tight Battle to Reach the Knockouts
narration.body: Türkiye face a challenging road ahead, but their tournament is very much alive. Our group-stage model gives them a 47% chance to reach the knockouts. They find themselves in a tough spot in Group D, with USA and Australia well clear at the top, leaving Türkiye to fight off Paraguay, who are right behind them with a 39% chance to advance. Following an opening 2-0 defeat to Australia, Türkiye are currently third in the group with zero points. In the wider tournament picture, they sit as the 12th-best third-placed team, just one goal behind the current cut-off to qualify. To secure their place in the Round of 32, finishing in the top two is their cleanest path, though that is currently a slim route. Instead, their most realistic hope is to claim a back-door spot as one of the best third-placed teams. To make that happen, they will likely need to target four points from their remaining two matches to keep their destiny in their own hands.

narration.headline: Tunisia Face an Uphill Climb After Opening Defeat
narration.body: Tunisia face a difficult road ahead in Group F after a tough opening match. Following their 5-1 defeat to Sweden, they sit at the bottom of the table with zero points and a challenging goal difference. They are currently adrift at the foot of the group, with a massive gap to make up on Japan, who sit comfortably ahead of them. Our tournament simulation gives Tunisia a 29% chance to reach the knockouts, meaning they face an outside chance of extending their stay. To turn things around, their cleanest route is to fight for a top-two finish, though that is currently a steep climb. A more realistic path to the Round of 32 may lie in scraping into third place and hoping to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, which remains a slim back-door possibility. With two matches left to play, everything is still mathematically open, but they will need to secure points quickly to keep their tournament hopes alive.

narration.headline: Sweden in Command of Group F After Flying Start
narration.body: Sweden are in a fantastic position to reach the knockouts, sitting comfortably clear at the top of Group F after their opening round. They are well clear of their closest rivals, with the Netherlands right behind them but trailing with a 76% chance to advance. Our tournament simulation gives Sweden a 94% chance to progress to the Round of 32. Having opened with a convincing 5-1 win over Tunisia, Sweden currently lead the group with three points. Their primary route is finishing in the top two, which is highly likely. They can put themselves in a very strong position to win the group—currently a strong possibility—by picking up four more points from their final two matches. Even if they slip up, they have a solid back-door route to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, though that path would leave them relying on results in other groups. For now, Sweden are firmly in control of their own destiny.

narration.headline: Switzerland on the Brink of the Knockouts
narration.body: Switzerland have put themselves in a fantastic position, with their place in the knockouts virtually assured. Our tournament simulation shows they have a 100% chance to reach the Round of 32. They are currently sitting second in Group B on four points, right behind Canada on goal difference. With Canada also sitting on a 100% chance to advance, the two sides are locked in a direct race for the top spot, while Bosnia and Herzegovina trail further back with a 41% chance. For the Swiss, the path forward is incredibly clean. Their primary route is finishing in the top two, which is essentially guaranteed. They can secure the top spot in the group—currently about a two-in-five chance—by winning their final match to reach seven points. Otherwise, they are highly likely to go through as the group runner-up, needing no help from other results. Even in the microscopic event they slip to third, they remain completely safe. With four points already in the bank, Switzerland have done the heavy lifting and hold all the cards.

narration.headline: Senegal Facing a Tight Battle to Reach the Knockouts
narration.body: Senegal face a real battle to reach the knockouts, with their tournament hopes sitting at about a coin flip. Following a 3-1 opening defeat to France, they find themselves in a challenging mid-table position in Group I. Norway and France have established a clear gap at the top, leaving Senegal to focus on keeping Iraq behind them to secure a path forward. Our group-stage model gives Senegal a 49% chance to advance to the Round of 32. While a direct top-two finish remains possible, it is currently a steep climb. Instead, their most likely route is leaning on the best-third back door, which is roughly a one-in-four chance. They currently sit as the tenth-best third-placed team, just one goal behind Ecuador for the final qualifying spot. To keep control of their destiny, Senegal realistically need to target four points from their final two matches to secure a strong third-place ranking, or push for two wins to leap back into the automatic qualification spots.

narration.headline: Scotland in Command of Group C After Perfect Start
narration.body: Scotland are in a fantastic position to reach the knockouts, sitting comfortably clear at the top of Group C after opening with a hard-fought 1-0 win over Haiti. Our tournament simulation gives Scotland a 90% chance to reach the Round of 32, putting them in a very strong position. While Brazil are their nearest rivals, the South Americans sit with daylight behind Scotland at a 78% advancement chance after drawing their opener with Morocco. With all four teams having played once, Scotland lead the group on three points. Their cleanest route is finishing in the top two, which is now a strong chance. They can likely secure this by taking another three points from their final two matches. Even if they slip up, there is a slim back-door route to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, though that would leave their fate in the hands of other groups. For now, Scotland are firmly in control of their own destiny.

narration.headline: South Africa Face an Uphill Climb but Remain in Touch
narration.body: South Africa face a difficult path to reach the knockouts, but their tournament is far from over. With two rounds complete, they sit at the bottom of Group A with one point, yet they remain within striking distance of their closest rivals. Czech Republic are right on their heels with a 34% chance to advance, keeping South Africa very much in touch in this tight race. Our tournament simulation gives South Africa a 32% chance to reach the Round of 32. Their cleanest route is to bypass the third-place math entirely by finishing as the group runner-up, which is currently about a one-in-four shot. To do that, they will likely need to secure a win in their final group match to reach four points. If they finish third, they have a slim back-door route to advance, but that path would leave them at the mercy of results in other groups. For now, their fate is still in their own hands.

narration.headline: Qatar face an uphill climb to keep knockout hopes alive
narration.body: Qatar find themselves in a tough spot at the bottom of Group B, chasing Bosnia and Herzegovina as the primary team to catch. With Bosnia and Herzegovina sitting on a 41% chance to advance, Qatar are still in the hunt but face a steep climb to reach the knockouts. Our tournament simulation gives them a 32% chance to keep their journey going. Having played two matches, Qatar sit fourth in the group with one point, level with Bosnia and Herzegovina but trailing on goal difference. Because Canada and Switzerland have already secured their places in the next round, Qatar's direct route to a top-two finish is virtually closed. Their only realistic path forward is to secure a win in their final match and hope to slip through the back door as one of the best third-placed teams. That route is not entirely in their hands, as it will depend on how results fall in other groups, but a victory keeps them in the conversation.

narration.headline: Portugal Well Placed in Group K After Opening Draw
narration.body: Portugal are in a strong position to reach the knockouts, with a 71% chance to advance to the Round of 32. Following their opening 1-1 draw with Congo DR, they sit third in Group K on one point. They are chasing Colombia, who lead the group with three points, while Congo DR remains right on their heels. Portugal's cleanest path is finishing in the top two, which our tournament simulation rates as more likely than not. If they fall short of the top two, they still have a slim back-door route to qualify as one of the best third-placed teams. They currently sit as the fourth-best third-placed team across the tournament, one point clear of the cut-off. To secure their spot without relying on other groups, Portugal will likely need at least four more points from their remaining matches against Colombia and Uzbekistan.

narration.headline: Paraguay Face an Uphill Climb After Opening Defeat
narration.body: Paraguay have a real fight on their hands to reach the knockouts, but their tournament is far from over. Following a tough 4-1 opening loss to the USA, they sit at the bottom of Group D without a point. They are currently chasing the pack, with Türkiye sitting just ahead of them as the immediate target to catch. While the USA and Australia have taken early control of the group, Türkiye's 46% advancement chance keeps them within reach if Paraguay can turn things around quickly. Our tournament simulation gives Paraguay a 39% chance to reach the Round of 32. Because of that opening defeat, a direct top-two finish is now a difficult route, with the odds of securing an automatic spot sitting at roughly a one-in-five shot. Instead, their most realistic path forward is likely the third-place back door. They have about a coin-flip chance of keeping their hopes alive through that route, which would require them to secure at least four points from their remaining matches and hope their record holds up against third-place teams in other groups.

Following their opening 1-0 loss to Ghana, they find themselves adrift at the bottom of Group L, with a real gap to close on Croatia, who sit just ahead of them with a 58% chance of moving on. With England and Ghana currently leading the group on three points each, Panama are in third place with zero points. Their cleanest route is finishing in the top two, but that is currently an outside chance. Instead, their most realistic path is fighting to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, which is roughly a coin flip if they can secure three points from their remaining matches. They currently sit as the ninth-best third-placed team across the tournament, just one spot outside the qualifying zone. While they are up against it, their fate is not yet sealed, and a strong result in their next match could quickly change the picture.

While they technically sit at the bottom of our simulation's advancement projections, they are right in touch with the rest of the pack. They are comfortably clear at the top of Group A, with South Korea their nearest rival at a 79% advancement chance, but there is clear daylight between Mexico and the rest of the chasing pack. Because they have won both of their matches—including a 1-0 victory over South Korea and a 2-0 win over South Africa—Mexico are guaranteed to finish in the top two. narration.headline: Japan Well Placed in Group F After Opening Draw
narration.body: Japan are in a strong position to reach the knockouts, with their fate very much in their own hands. Following a hard-fought 2-2 draw with the Netherlands, Japan sit second in Group F with one point. They are right in the mix in a tight race for the top spots, with the Netherlands right on their heels at 76% to advance, while Sweden lead the group after the first round of matches. Our tournament simulation gives Japan a 74% chance to reach the Round of 32. Finishing in the top two remains their cleanest path forward, which is currently more likely than not. If they slip, they still hold a slim back-door route to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, though that path would leave them at the mercy of results in other groups. To keep control, Japan will look to secure maximum points from their remaining two matches to lock down a top-two spot.

narration.headline: Jordan Facing an Uphill Battle but Still in Touch
narration.body: Jordan face a challenging road ahead, but their knockout hopes are far from over. Following an opening 3-1 defeat to Austria, our tournament simulation gives Jordan a 36% chance to reach the knockouts. They currently sit at the bottom of Group J but remain within striking distance of Algeria, who are just ahead of them with a 40% chance of advancing. With Argentina and Austria commanding the top two spots, Jordan's cleanest path to the Round of 32 is a steep climb. A top-two finish is unlikely, meaning their most realistic route is fighting to advance as one of the best third-placed teams. They currently rank as the 11th-best third-place side, sitting just one goal behind Ecuador for the final qualifying spot. To keep this back-door route alive, Jordan will likely need to secure at least three points from their remaining two matches and hope other group results fall their way.

narration.headline: Iraq Face an Uphill Climb in Group I
narration.body: Iraq face a difficult road ahead after opening with a 4-1 defeat to Norway. They are currently sitting at the bottom of Group I with zero points and a minus-three goal difference, leaving them with an outside chance of 30% to reach the knockouts. They find themselves adrift at the foot of the group, chasing Senegal who sit just above them with a 49% chance to advance. With powerhouse sides Norway and France looking comfortable at the top, Iraq's cleanest route to the Round of 32—finishing in the top two—is highly unlikely. Instead, their most realistic path is to fight for third place and hope to squeeze through the back door as one of the best third-placed teams. To make that happen, they will likely need to find at least three points from their remaining two matches against France and Senegal, while hoping results in other groups fall their way. The mountain is steep, but with two matches left, their tournament is still very much alive.

narration.headline: Iran Well Placed in a Tight Group G Scramble
narration.body: Iran are in a decent position to reach the knockouts, but they are right in the thick of a highly congested race. In Group G, a tight cluster has formed where Belgium sit just ahead with a 71% chance to advance, while Egypt are right on Iran's heels at 65%. Following a 2-2 draw with New Zealand in their opening match, Iran currently sit second in the group with one point. Our tournament simulation gives Iran a 66% chance to reach the Round of 32. Their primary route is finishing in the top two, which is more likely than not to happen. If they fall into third place, they still have a slim back-door route to advance, though that path would depend on results in other groups. To keep control of their destiny, Iran will likely need to secure at least three to four more points from their final two group matches.

narration.headline: Haiti Face an Uphill Climb After Opening Defeat
narration.body: Haiti face a challenging road ahead after opening their tournament with a narrow 1-0 loss to Scotland. They find themselves at the foot of Group C, with real ground to make up on Morocco, who sit comfortably ahead with a 71% chance to advance. Our tournament simulation gives Haiti a 34% chance to reach the knockouts, meaning they face an uphill battle but remain very much in the fight. Finishing in the top two is a steep climb, with the odds of securing an automatic spot currently sitting at less than one-in-five. Instead, their most realistic path to the Round of 32 may lie in the best third-place route, which is roughly a coin flip compared to their direct qualification chances. To keep their hopes alive, Haiti will likely need to target at least three points from their remaining two matches to put themselves in the conversation for a wild-card spot, though that route would ultimately depend on how results fall in other groups.

narration.headline: Germany in Command After Opening Statement
narration.body: Germany are in a fantastic position to reach the knockouts, sitting comfortably at the top of Group E after their opening match. They hold a strong lead in the group race, though Ivory Coast remain right on their heels with an 89% chance of advancing themselves. Our tournament simulation gives Germany a 97% chance to progress to the Round of 32, with their primary route being a top-two finish. They are heavily favoured to secure that direct path, which is very much in their own hands. Even if they slip up, there is a slim back-door route to advance as one of the best third-placed teams. Following their dominant 7-1 opening victory over Curaçao, Germany sit first in the standings with three points and a massive goal difference. While they are not yet mathematically guaranteed a spot, they are in a very strong position heading into the remaining group fixtures.

narration.headline: France in Command After Opening Win
narration.body: France are in a very strong position to reach the knockouts, opening their tournament with a commanding 3-1 win over Senegal. The prediction system gives them a 94% chance to reach the Round of 32. They are locked in a tight race at the top of Group I with Norway, who sit just ahead on goal difference with a 95% chance to advance. France are heavily expected to secure their passage through a top-two finish, which remains entirely in their own hands. Winning the group is their most likely path, with about a one-in-two chance of taking the top spot. If they slip up, they have a strong runner-up safety net, while the back-door route of advancing as a best third-placed team remains a slim fallback. With three points already secured, France are well on their way, but they will need to keep their foot on the gas with two group matches left to play.

narration.headline: Egypt Well Placed in Tight Group G Scramble
narration.body: Egypt are in a promising position to reach the knockouts, with our tournament simulation giving them a 66% chance to advance to the Round of 32. They find themselves in the thick of a highly congested Group G race, with Iran just ahead of them at 66% and New Zealand right on their heels at 60%. Following a 1-1 draw with Belgium in their opening match, Egypt are currently fourth in the group on goals scored, but with all four teams sitting on one point, the group is completely level. Finishing in the top two remains a real chance, roughly a coin flip at 47%, which would guarantee safe passage. If they miss out on those top spots, they have a slim back-door route to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, though that path would leave their fate in the hands of results in other groups. To secure their place, Egypt will likely need to target at least three or four more points from their final two matches.

narration.body: Ecuador are in a decent position to reach the knockouts, with our tournament simulation giving them a 61% chance to advance. They currently sit third in Group E after an opening 1-0 loss to Ivory Coast, who are comfortably ahead in second with an 89% chance of going through. Ecuador remain well clear of Curaçao, but they have work to do to catch the frontrunners. Finishing in the top two is an uphill climb, with that direct route now looking like an outside chance. Instead, Ecuador's most likely path is squeezing through the back door as one of the best third-placed teams. They currently sit as the eighth-best third-place team, right on the cut-off line, level on points and goal difference with Panama. To secure their spot in the Round of 32, Ecuador realistically need to target three points from their remaining two matches. While their fate is no longer entirely in their own hands and depends on how other groups shake out, a strong performance in their next match will keep them firmly in the mix.

narration.body: The Czech Republic face a challenging path to reach the knockouts, with our tournament simulation giving them a 34% chance to advance. They are currently locked in a tight scrap at the bottom of Group A, holding off South Africa who are pressing right behind them, while South Korea remain well clear in second place with a 79% chance to go through. They currently sit as the sixth-best third-placed team, just one point above the cut-off, but this provisional position remains highly vulnerable. To keep their tournament alive, they realistically need to secure a win in their final group match and hope results in other groups fall their way.

Following a heavy opening defeat to Germany, they sit at the bottom of the table with two matches left to play. narration.body: Argentina are in a brilliant position to reach the knockouts, carrying a 96% chance of advancing to the Round of 32. They sit top of Group J after opening with a convincing 3-0 win over Algeria, but the race remains tight with Austria right on their heels at 92%. Their primary route is clear: finishing in the top two is highly likely, with a strong chance to win the group outright. If they hit a bump, a slim back-door route remains open to advance as one of the best third-placed teams, though that path would leave their fate in the hands of other groups. For now, Argentina are firmly in control of their own destiny.

narration.body: Algeria face a tense battle to keep their tournament alive after a tough opening match. Our prediction system gives them a 40% chance to reach the knockouts, meaning they are in a difficult spot but still very much in the fight. They are currently sitting at the bottom of Group J with zero points, while Argentina and Austria have taken early control. narration.body: Ghana have put themselves in a strong position to reach the knockouts, opening their tournament with a vital win. With three points on the board, our tournament simulation gives the Black Stars a 76% chance to reach the Round of 32. They currently sit second in Group L, chasing England who lead on goal difference, while keeping a comfortable cushion over Croatia, who sit further back with a 58% chance to advance. Our tournament simulation gives them a 94% chance to reach the Round of 32. They sit comfortably clear at the top of Group L after an entertaining 4-2 victory over Croatia, with nearest rivals Ghana, who opened with a 1-0 win over Panama, sitting just behind them on goal difference but trailing significantly in overall advancement probability. Following their opening 1-1 draw with Morocco, Brazil are currently third in the group standings with one point. They also sit as the second-best third-placed team across the tournament, one point clear of the cut-off. Brazil's cleanest route is finishing in the top two, which is more likely than not to happen. To secure that direct path, they typically need to find about three more points from their final two matches. If they slip to third, they still have a slim back-door route to qualify, though that path leaves their fate in the hands of other groups.

They currently lead a tight Group H race, with Uruguay right on their heels with a 69% chance to go through. Following an opening scoreless draw with Cape Verde Islands, Spain sit third in a completely level group where every team has one point. Their cleanest route is finishing in the top two, which our tournament simulation rates as more likely than not. If they slip to third, they still have a slim back-door route to qualify. They currently sit as the fifth-best third-place team across the tournament, one point clear of the cut-off, though that cross-group comparison remains provisional. To secure their spot without relying on other groups, Spain will look to claim about three more points from their final two matches to lock down a top-two finish.

narration.headline: Canada on the Brink of the Knockout Stage
narration.body: Canada are in a fantastic position, with their place in the knockouts virtually assured. Our tournament simulation shows they have a 100% chance to reach the Round of 32. They currently sit top of Group B with four points after two matches, but they cannot afford to relax just yet. Switzerland are right on their heels, also sitting on four points and sharing a 100% chance to advance, making the race for the top spot incredibly tight. Canada's primary route is finishing in the top two, which is almost a certainty. Winning the group remains the main target, with roughly a three-in-five chance of doing so, while finishing as the runner-up is a very strong backup possibility. Because they are in such a commanding position, they do not need to worry about the complicated best-third back door. With one round of games left to play, Canada are in complete control of their own destiny as they look to secure their place in the next round.
